channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: At War With Iran Again, Trump Finds an Opponent He Cannot Easily Dominate
item.description: The president is accustomed to forcing other countries to bend to his will. But he is struggling to come up with a strategy to extract Iranian concessions as the cease-fire he brokered collapses.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A banner in Tehran last week threatening President Trump.

item.title: Iran’s Cyberattackers Tracked Phones of U.S. Military Personnel in the Mideast, Data Suggests
item.description: Researchers say Iran’s cyberwarfare capabilities have grown more advanced and aggressive during the war with the United States and Israel.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iran used cellphone signals to track U.S. military members at the start of the war, according to newly released data.

item.title: Trump Drops Plan to Tax Ships in Strait of Hormuz as Fighting with Iran Escalates
item.description: The president’s proposal to impose a fee on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz threatened to increase global energy prices.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Jenny Gross and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump scrapped a plan on Tuesday to impose a 20 percent fee on cargo passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: ‘We Love Iraq,’ Trump Tells New Prime Minister in Oval Office Meeting
item.description: The meeting comes as President Trump’s administration has been ratcheting up pressure on Baghdad to diminish Iran’s influence in Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: “We’re going to be doing a lot of deals,” President Trump said as he met with Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi in the Oval Office on Tuesday.

item.title: Iran’s Former Leader Denies Times Report
item.description: The Times reported that Israel had planned to reinstall Mahmoud Ahmadinejad as Iran’s leader after the war.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, the former president of Iran, during a news conference in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Israel Strikes Police Post, Killing 7, Gaza Officials Say
item.description: Hamas says the strike hit Gaza’s civil security, while Israel says it targeted “terrorists.” Israel has stepped up attacks in Gaza recently as a cease-fire with Hamas has floundered.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.media:description: The bodies of some of those killed in an Israeli strike on a police station on Tuesday in Jabaliya, in northern Gaza.

item.title: Trump Flip-Flops on Strait of Hormuz, Creating New Risks for Global Economy
item.description: The president now wants Gulf nations to invest in the U.S. in exchange for safe passage. Shipping industry experts worry other nations will follow that lead.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: A day after saying he would charge 20 percent fees to ships in exchange for U.S. military protection in the Strait of Hormuz, Mr. Trump said he wanted Gulf states to invest in the United States instead.

item.title: India Condemns Deadly Iranian Strike on Commercial Ship
item.description: India issued a diplomatic protest after an Indian sailor was killed and dozens more were injured in an Iranian attack on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu and Mujib Mashal
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An Indian-flagged tanker carrying crude oil that transited through the Strait of Hormuz, offloading at a terminal near Mumbai in April.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon Meet to Advance Peace Talks, as Broader Truce Unravels
item.description: Expectations are low for the latest round of U.S.-mediated talks, aimed at ending months of war between Israel and t Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The U.S. Embassy in Rome, Italy, as U.S.-mediated talks between Israel and Lebanon resumed on Tuesday.

item.title: Jeffries Says He Will Oppose Proposal to Cut Off U.S. Aid to Israel
item.description: The House Democratic leader said he would not seek to persuade other Democrats to vote against the measure, which has divided the party amid a deep rift over support for Israel.
item.dc:creator: Annie Karni
item.media:description: Representative Hakeem Jeffries at the Capitol on Monday.

item.title: Europe, Flexing Muscle in Ukraine, Is Still on Sidelines in Iran
item.description: In a show of collective strength, President Emmanuel Macron of France hosted European allies at the annual Bastille Day parade in Paris. Yet Europe is still a hostage to America’s foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vehicles of the French Army’s Ninth Marine Infantry Brigade on the Champs-Élysées during the annual Bastille Day military parade in Paris on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump’s Canceled Plan to Charge a Toll in the Strait of Hormuz: What to Know
item.description: A day after President Trump announced a hefty 20 percent fee on cargo through the waterway, he reversed his decision.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil and Yan Zhuang
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump on Tuesday retreated from his plan to levy a toll on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz as a way for the United States to recover the cost of providing military protection to vessels using the waterway.

item.title: Oil Price Dips From High After Trump Changes Plans for Shipping Fees
item.description: Brent crude, the international benchmark, breached $85 per barrel for the first time in a month.
item.dc:creator: Jason Karaian, Katie Robertson and Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Max Bearak and Ishani Desai

item.title: Hamas Forces Interfered With Aid Distribution, U.N. Says
item.description: A raid by armed personnel led the World Food Program to suspend its operations in one part of Gaza, the U.N. said. Hamas disputed the accusations.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Adam Rasgon
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Israel’s war against Hamas has left homes largely destroyed in Khan Younis in southern Gaza.

item.title: Trump Reimposes Naval Blockade, as U.S. and Iran Escalate Strikes
item.description: After several days of attacks, Tehran and Washington have effectively reverted to the state of open conflict that existed before the United States and Iran agreed to a cease-fire in June.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Leo Sands and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships sailing near the Strait of Hormuz off the eastern coast of the United Arab Emirates at Khor Fakkan on Monday.

item.title: In a First, U.S. Forces Used Naval Drones in Combat Operations
item.description: The use of unmanned vessels to attack Iranian naval installations offers new evidence of how drones are taking over modern warfare.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump’s Threat to Impose Fees in the Strait of Hormuz Contradicts His Aides
item.description: President Trump said the United States could collect tolls or fees, despite Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio saying no country could do so.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships sailing near the Strait of Hormuz off the eastern coast of the United Arab Emirates at Khor Fakkan on Monday.

item.title: Yemen’s Fragile Peace in Peril as Houthis Accuse Saudi Arabia of Bombing
item.description: The Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen accused Saudi Arabia of striking Yemen’s main international airport in a dispute over an Iranian plane that tried to land in the capital, Sana.
item.dc:creator: Shuaib Almosawa and Vivian Nereim
item.media:description: Smoke billowed from the area of the main airport in Sana, Yemen, on Monday.

item.title: Iranian Hard-Liners Threaten Trump as U.S.-Iran Strikes Continue
item.description: Some Iranians are calling for revenge against President Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel for the February killing the country’s supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s casket along with other family members during the funeral procession in Tehran last week.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Ship Traffic Falls to Lowest Point in a Month After Strikes
item.description: Shipping activity in the critical waterway has plunged after Iran attacked a vessel, setting off U.S. strikes. President Trump said he would reinstate a blockade of Iranian ports.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s where the U.S. and Iran are targeting each other in their latest attacks.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Hari Raj
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from the Musandam Peninsula, Oman, on Sunday.

item.title: Lebanese Hold Fast to Their Land Despite Threat of Long Israeli Occupation
item.description: Entire towns in southern Lebanon have been hollowed out by the war between Israel and Hezbollah. Some residents have stayed, fearing permanent displacement.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Daniel Berehulak
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Soldiers escorted Haneen Sayed, the minister of social affairs, through the town of Arqoub in southern Lebanon in May.

item.title: Inside Israel’s Secret Operation to Install Ahmadinejad, Iran’s Former President, as Leader
item.description: The yearslong effort to groom the former Iranian president as an intelligence asset culminated in a dramatic effort to take him to a Mossad safe house in Iran in the early days of the war. But the plan fell apart.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, Iran’s former leader, was known for accelerating Iran’s nuclear program and his anti-Israel views.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt and Richard Pérez-Peña

item.title: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa, Ruler Who Transformed Qatar, Dies at 74
item.description: Under Sheikh Hamad, the tiny nation of Qatar developed immense natural gas wealth and cultivated influence far beyond its borders.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.media:description: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani is often described as the founding father of modern Qatar.

item.title: Lindsey Graham Championed Israel, Even as Wars Eroded American Support
item.description: The Republican senator from South Carolina had an intense interest in the Middle East, and was a staunch supporter of U.S. strikes on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Senator Lindsey Graham, in 2024 in Washington, leading a Republican news conference to condemn the Biden administration’s decision to pause the delivery of bombs to Israel.

item.title: How Trump Failed to Secure the Strait of Hormuz in His Iran Deal
item.description: President Trump signed an agreement that Iran said gave it control of the waterway — and global energy supplies. Now, Iran’s military is violently asserting authority.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Michael Crowley and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump at Joint Base Andrews on Thursday. He recently said the memorandum of understanding reached with Iran was “over.”

item.title: Here’s a look at the heavy strikes in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang, Aaron Boxerman and Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam Peninsula, Oman, on Sunday. Oman said drones had been fired at sites in the area on Sunday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Aaron Boxerman, Erica L. Green, Sanam Mahoozi and Hari Raj

item.title: Hard-Liners in Iran Want to Keep Fighting America
item.description: With much of their leadership killed in the war, Iran’s conservatives have sought to fill the void and intensify the fight against the United States.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourners in Mashhad, Iran, during Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s funeral, on Thursday.

item.title: Oil Prices Set for More Volatility After Latest Iranian Attack in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Crude oil prices jumped above prewar levels this week, demonstrating Iran’s capacity to move energy prices by projecting power in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Jason Karaian
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Boats anchored off Oman’s northern Musandam Peninsula near the Strait of Hormuz in June.

item.title: Here’s a look at the heavy strikes in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.media:description: A vessel is seen in the Persian Gulf off the coast of Ajman, United Arab Emirates, on Friday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar, Shirin Hakim, Aaron Boxerman and Yan Zhuang

item.title: U.S. Strikes Iran After Iran Fires on Ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran’s attack on a container ship came hours after a meeting between the foreign ministers of Iran and Oman about opening the strait to traffic.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman and Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz seen from Musandam, Oman, this month. While the Trump administration has said that a cease-fire would fully lift Iran’s blockade of the strait, that has yet to be borne out.

item.title: Israel Struck an Iranian Steel Facility. Was It a Valid Military Target?
item.description: During the war, Israel attacked Iran’s steel plants, saying they provided forces with revenue and the means to make weapons, but it also hurt the civilian economy.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Shippers Face Deepening Dilemma as U.S. and Iran Vie for Control of the Strait
item.description: Companies desperately want to get their ships moving through the Strait of Hormuz, but face mounting risks. “Things are becoming uglier by the minute,” one executive said.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Jenny Gross and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ships anchored off Oman’s coast near the Strait of Hormuz. Ship operators in the area are eager to resume normal business but face great peril if they do.

item.title: Trump Says Negotiations With Iran Will Continue as U.S. Ramps Up Strikes
item.description: U.S. forces hit more than 170 targets, including air defense systems, drone and missile storage sites, and military speedboats.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: The funeral procession in Tehran on Thursday for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s former leader, who was killed in February during U.S. and Israeli military strikes on his country.

item.title: Dinaw Mengestu Resigns as PEN America President After Israel Article
item.description: The novelist Dinaw Mengestu, who was elected seven months ago, said the article “continues this approach toward defending some rights while not defending others.”
item.dc:creator: Malia Mendez
item.media:description: Dinaw Mengestu had been on PEN America’s board for about a decade before he became its president in December.

item.title: Mediators Trying to Pull U.S. and Iran Back From Brink, Officials Say
item.description: Qatari mediators were in Iran seeking to salvage the tattered truce. President Trump said talks with Tehran would continue, even if the cease-fire was “over.”
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman, on Thursday.

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Remains Absent, a Void at the Top of the Regime
item.description: Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei did not appear at his father’s funeral this week, fueling speculation about his physical condition and leaving a power vacuum in a divided country.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pro-government demonstration in Tehran in May, with a banner showing Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: Bombing Iran Didn’t Work for Trump. Neither Did a Tentative Cease-Fire. Is There a Plan C?
item.description: The administration appears to be reverting to an all-stick, no-carrot approach. But it has yet to answer why it believes economic warfare and bombing will yield a different result this time.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Monday. The United States has bombed more than 170 Iranian military targets over two nights this week as a cease-fire unraveled.

item.title: Iranian Attacks on Jordan Highlight Kingdom’s Difficult Position
item.description: Iran’s military said it fired missiles at a Jordanian air base used by American forces. Jordan said it had intercepted missiles entering its air space.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A satellite image shows Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Al Azraq, Jordan.

item.title: As U.S. Steps Up Attacks on Iran, Prospect of All-Out War Rises
item.description: American officials said they were trying to safeguard maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran said it had responded by firing at U.S. bases in Qatar, Bahrain and Kuwait.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak, Yeganeh Torbati, Eric Schmitt and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Israel Investigating After Photo of Stripped and Bound Detainee Is Shared
item.description: The Israeli military confirmed the authenticity of the photo, which circulated widely after it was shared by a Palestinian activist. Israeli rights groups said it depicted a war crime.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

item.title: Palestinian Authority Sets Date for First Major Elections in 20 Years
item.description: Analysts expressed skepticism that the vote would take place as planned. In 2021, Mahmoud Abbas, the authority’s leader, scheduled and then canceled a similar election.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Aaron Boxerman
item.media:description: Mahmoud Abbas, the president of the Palestinian Authority speaking during a conference in Ramallah, in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, in May. His term ended in 2009, but he has since canceled elections.

item.title: In Exchange of Strikes With Iran, U.S. Lands Most of the Blows
item.description: This difference reflects Iran’s limited firepower and degraded air defenses, and highlights how the war is taking a disproportionate toll on Iranians.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A screen grab from a social media video showing the aftermath of a strike on Wednesday in Kuhestak, Iran.

item.title: Iranian port cities and a railway were struck overnight.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and James McManagan
item.media:description: Social media, via Reuters
